title: Oritrophium limnophilum
taxonomy: Oritrophium limnophilum belongs to the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the order Asterales, a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as the sunflower family. Oritrophium limnophilum is a species of flowering plant that is native to the high-altitude regions of South America. It is further categorized into the tribe Millerieae, a group of plants that are characterized by their daisy-like flowers and asteraceous inflorescences. The exact classification of Oritrophium limnophilum is still a subject of ongoing research and may be subject to revision in the future.
uses: Oritrophium limnophilum, also known as the mountain sunflower, has been found to have potential medicinal uses. Its properties have been studied for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which could be beneficial in treating various health conditions. Additionally, its leaves and flowers are sometimes used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues and fever.
